pillars.name: Stellar Structure & Evolution
pillars.description: The physics of stars: hydrostatic and thermal balance, energy generation by fusion, the main sequence, and the evolution of stars from birth to their final states.
pillars.descriptionCs: Fyzika hvězd: hydrostatická a tepelná rovnováha, uvolňování energie fúzí, hlavní posloupnost a vývoj hvězd od zrození po jejich konečné stavy.

blocks.name: Stellar Structure & Energy Generation
blocks.description: A star is a self-gravitating ball of plasma in hydrostatic equilibrium, its inward gravity balanced by outward pressure sustained by nuclear fusion in the core; energy flows outward by radiation and convection to the surface.
blocks.descriptionCs: Hvězda je samogravitující koule plazmatu v hydrostatické rovnováze, jejíž dovnitř mířící gravitaci vyvažuje ven mířící tlak udržovaný jadernou fúzí v jádru; energie proudí ven zářením a konvekcí k povrchu.

brief.name: Hydrostatic equilibrium
brief.definition: The balance between gravity and pressure that keeps a star stable.

brief.name: Proton-proton chain
brief.definition: The dominant hydrogen-fusion sequence powering low-mass stars.

brief.name: CNO cycle
brief.definition: A catalytic hydrogen-fusion cycle dominant in more massive stars.

brief.name: Radiative and convective transport
brief.definition: The two ways energy moves from a star's core to its surface.

brief.name: Luminosity
brief.definition: The total power a star radiates, set by its mass and structure.

blocks.name: Stellar Evolution
blocks.description: Stars evolve as they exhaust their nuclear fuel, moving off the main sequence to become giants and shedding or exploding their outer layers; the mass of a star determines its lifetime and its final fate.
blocks.descriptionCs: Hvězdy se vyvíjejí, jak spotřebovávají jaderné palivo, opouštějí hlavní posloupnost, stávají se obry a odvrhávají nebo vybuchují své vnější vrstvy; hmotnost hvězdy určuje její délku života i konečný osud.

brief.name: Main sequence
brief.definition: The long stable phase of core hydrogen fusion where most stars spend their lives.

brief.name: Hertzsprung-Russell diagram
brief.definition: The plot of luminosity against temperature mapping stellar populations.

brief.name: Red giant phase
brief.definition: The swelling of a star after core hydrogen is exhausted.

brief.name: Planetary nebula
brief.definition: The ejected envelope of a dying low-mass star, leaving a white dwarf.

brief.name: Mass-lifetime relation
brief.definition: The rule that more massive stars burn brighter and die far sooner.

pillars.name: Compact Objects
pillars.description: The dense remnants of stellar death: white dwarfs, neutron stars and stellar black holes, supported against gravity by degeneracy pressure or not at all.
pillars.descriptionCs: Husté pozůstatky zániku hvězd: bílí trpaslíci, neutronové hvězdy a hvězdné černé díry, držené proti gravitaci degeneračním tlakem, nebo vůbec ne.

blocks.name: White Dwarfs & Neutron Stars
blocks.description: White dwarfs and neutron stars are the collapsed cores of dead stars, held up by electron and neutron degeneracy pressure respectively; each has a maximum mass beyond which collapse continues, the Chandrasekhar and Tolman-Oppenheimer-Volkoff limits.
blocks.descriptionCs: Bílí trpaslíci a neutronové hvězdy jsou zhroucená jádra mrtvých hvězd, držená degeneračním tlakem elektronů, respektive neutronů; každý má maximální hmotnost, za níž kolaps pokračuje — Chandrasekharovu a Tolmanovu–Oppenheimerovu–Volkoffovu mez.

brief.name: Degeneracy pressure
brief.definition: The quantum pressure from the exclusion principle supporting compact stars.

brief.name: Chandrasekhar limit
brief.definition: The maximum mass of a white dwarf, about 1.4 solar masses.

brief.name: Neutron star
brief.definition: An ultradense stellar remnant composed largely of neutrons.

brief.name: Pulsar
brief.definition: A rapidly rotating magnetised neutron star seen as pulses of radiation.

brief.name: Tolman-Oppenheimer-Volkoff limit
brief.definition: The maximum mass of a neutron star before black-hole collapse.

blocks.name: Stellar Black Holes & Accretion
blocks.description: When collapse exceeds all support a stellar black hole forms; matter falling onto compact objects releases enormous energy as it forms a hot accretion disc, making X-ray binaries and quasars among the brightest sources in the sky.
blocks.descriptionCs: Když kolaps překročí veškerou oporu, vzniká hvězdná černá díra; hmota padající na kompaktní objekty uvolňuje při tvorbě horkého akrečního disku obrovskou energii, což činí z rentgenových dvojhvězd a kvasarů jedny z nejjasnějších zdrojů na obloze.

brief.name: Stellar black hole
brief.definition: A black hole formed by the collapse of a massive star's core.

brief.name: Accretion disc
brief.definition: A disc of infalling matter heated to radiate as it spirals inward.

brief.name: X-ray binary
brief.definition: A compact object accreting from a companion star, bright in X-rays.

brief.name: Relativistic jet
brief.definition: A collimated outflow launched from near an accreting compact object.

brief.name: Eddington limit
brief.definition: The luminosity at which radiation pressure halts further accretion.

pillars.name: Interstellar Medium & Star Formation
pillars.description: The gas and dust between the stars and the collapse that turns it into new stars: molecular clouds, gravitational instability, and the physics of accretion and protostars.
pillars.descriptionCs: Plyn a prach mezi hvězdami a kolaps, který jej mění v nové hvězdy: molekulární mračna, gravitační nestabilita a fyzika akrece a protohvězd.

blocks.name: The Interstellar Medium
blocks.description: The space between stars holds gas and dust in phases from cold molecular clouds to hot ionised plasma, enriched by stellar death and cycled into new stars; its physics governs cooling, heating and the chemistry of space.
blocks.descriptionCs: Prostor mezi hvězdami obsahuje plyn a prach ve fázích od chladných molekulárních mračen po horké ionizované plazma, obohacovaný zánikem hvězd a znovu zapojovaný do nových hvězd; jeho fyzika řídí ochlazování, ohřev a chemii vesmíru.

brief.name: Molecular cloud
brief.definition: A cold dense region of gas where stars form.

brief.name: Interstellar dust
brief.definition: Fine solid grains that redden starlight and seed molecule formation.

brief.name: Ionised gas
brief.definition: Hot plasma around massive stars, glowing as emission nebulae.

brief.name: Interstellar extinction
brief.definition: The dimming and reddening of starlight by intervening dust.

brief.name: Cosmic cycle of matter
brief.definition: The recycling of gas from dying stars into new generations.

blocks.name: Star Formation
blocks.description: Stars are born when a molecular cloud becomes gravitationally unstable and fragments and collapses, forming protostars that accrete through discs and ignite fusion once their cores reach the required temperature.
blocks.descriptionCs: Hvězdy se rodí, když se molekulární mračno stane gravitačně nestabilním, rozpadne se a zhroutí, přičemž vznikají protohvězdy, které akreují skrze disky a zažehnou fúzi, jakmile jejich jádra dosáhnou potřebné teploty.

brief.name: Jeans instability
brief.definition: The condition under which a gas cloud collapses under its own gravity.

brief.name: Protostar
brief.definition: A forming star still accreting from its birth cloud.

brief.name: Protoplanetary disc
brief.definition: The disc of gas and dust around a young star from which planets form.

brief.name: Initial mass function
brief.definition: The distribution of masses with which stars are born.

brief.name: Bipolar outflow
brief.definition: The jets of gas ejected along the poles of a forming star.

pillars.name: Galactic & Extragalactic Astrophysics
pillars.description: The structure and dynamics of galaxies and the largest systems: galactic rotation and dark matter, active galactic nuclei, and clusters and the cosmic web.
pillars.descriptionCs: Struktura a dynamika galaxií a největších soustav: rotace galaxií a temná hmota, aktivní galaktická jádra a kupy galaxií a kosmická síť.

blocks.name: Galaxy Dynamics & Dark Matter
blocks.description: Galaxies are gravitationally bound systems of stars, gas and dark matter; their flat rotation curves reveal far more mass than the visible stars provide, the primary evidence for dark matter haloes surrounding them.
blocks.descriptionCs: Galaxie jsou gravitačně vázané soustavy hvězd, plynu a temné hmoty; jejich ploché rotační křivky odhalují mnohem více hmoty, než poskytují viditelné hvězdy, což je hlavní důkaz pro halo temné hmoty, jež je obklopují.

brief.name: Rotation curve
brief.definition: The orbital speed of stars versus distance from a galaxy's centre.

brief.name: Dark matter halo
brief.definition: The extended invisible mass whose gravity binds a galaxy.

brief.name: Galaxy morphology
brief.definition: The classification of galaxies as spiral, elliptical or irregular.

brief.name: Galactic dynamics
brief.definition: The gravitational motion of stars and gas within a galaxy.

brief.name: Galaxy mergers
brief.definition: The collisions that reshape galaxies and trigger star formation.

blocks.name: Active Galactic Nuclei & Large-Scale Structure
blocks.description: A supermassive black hole accreting at a galaxy's centre powers active galactic nuclei and quasars, while galaxies themselves cluster into filaments and voids forming the cosmic web, the largest structure in the universe.
blocks.descriptionCs: Supermasivní černá díra akreující ve středu galaxie pohání aktivní galaktická jádra a kvasary, zatímco samotné galaxie se shlukují do vláken a prázdnot tvořících kosmickou síť, největší strukturu ve vesmíru.

brief.name: Supermassive black hole
brief.definition: The million-to-billion-solar-mass black hole at a galaxy's core.

brief.name: Quasar
brief.definition: An extremely luminous active galactic nucleus in the distant universe.

brief.name: Galaxy cluster
brief.definition: A gravitationally bound assembly of hundreds to thousands of galaxies.

brief.name: Cosmic web
brief.definition: The filamentary large-scale arrangement of galaxies and dark matter.

brief.name: Gravitational lensing
brief.definition: The bending of light by mass, used to map dark matter and magnify distant sources.

pillars.name: High-Energy & Multi-Messenger Astrophysics
pillars.description: The most violent phenomena and the many channels that reveal them: supernovae and their remnants, cosmic rays, gamma-ray bursts, and observation through light, particles and gravitational waves.
pillars.descriptionCs: Nejprudší jevy a mnoho kanálů, jimiž se projevují: supernovy a jejich pozůstatky, kosmické záření, záblesky gama a pozorování světlem, částicemi a gravitačními vlnami.

blocks.name: Supernovae & Cosmic Rays
blocks.description: Massive stars end in supernova explosions that disperse newly forged elements and drive shock waves accelerating cosmic rays — high-energy particles that pervade the galaxy and strike the Earth's atmosphere.
blocks.descriptionCs: Hmotné hvězdy končí výbuchy supernov, které rozptylují nově vytvořené prvky a vytvářejí rázové vlny urychlující kosmické záření — vysokoenergetické částice, jež prostupují galaxií a dopadají na zemskou atmosféru.

brief.name: Core-collapse supernova
brief.definition: The explosion of a massive star when its iron core collapses.

brief.name: Type Ia supernova
brief.definition: The thermonuclear detonation of a white dwarf, a cosmic standard candle.

brief.name: Supernova remnant
brief.definition: The expanding shocked shell left by a supernova.

brief.name: Cosmic rays
brief.definition: High-energy charged particles from space, accelerated by shocks and other sources.

brief.name: Shock acceleration
brief.definition: The mechanism boosting particles to high energy at astrophysical shock fronts.

blocks.name: Multi-Messenger Astrophysics
blocks.description: The universe is now observed through several messengers — electromagnetic radiation across the spectrum, neutrinos, cosmic rays and gravitational waves — whose combination pinpoints events such as neutron-star mergers and gamma-ray bursts.
blocks.descriptionCs: Vesmír se nyní pozoruje prostřednictvím několika poslů — elektromagnetického záření napříč spektrem, neutrin, kosmického záření a gravitačních vln — jejichž kombinace přesně určuje děje, jako je splývání neutronových hvězd a záblesky gama.

brief.name: Gamma-ray burst
brief.definition: The brightest electromagnetic explosion, from collapsing stars or merging compact objects.

brief.name: Neutrino astronomy
brief.definition: Observation of astrophysical neutrinos that escape dense sources.

brief.name: Gravitational-wave astronomy
brief.definition: The detection of spacetime ripples from merging compact objects.

brief.name: Kilonova
brief.definition: The optical glow of a neutron-star merger forging heavy elements.

brief.name: Messenger coincidence
brief.definition: The joint detection of one event across multiple channels, localising its source.
